rules.description:
"In a Challenge, Fulgrim's Attacks Characteristic is modified if his Initiative Characteristic is higher than his opponent's."


While a Model with this Special Rule is taking part in a Challenge, if its Controlling Player has Challenge Advantage, modify the Attacks Characteristic of the Model with this Special Rule by +1 for each point that its Base Initiative Characteristic is higher than its opponent's Bas Initiative Characteristic.
rules.name: Sublime Swordsman

rules.description:
If a Model with this Special Rule is part of an Army, then the Controlling Player may apply the following additional Special Rules to all Auxiliary, Apex and Primary Detachments that have the same Faction Trait as the Model with this Special Rule:

• If four or more Troops Battlefield Role Slots in the Primary Detachment of this Army are filled with Tactical Squad Units and/or Assault Squad Units, then all Force Organisation Slots with the Troops Battlefield Role in the Primary Detachment are considered Prime Slots.

Furthermore, if a Model with this Special Rule is part of an Army, then the following Special Rules are granted to all Models with the Emperor's Children Trait in the same Army:

• A Unit that includes only Models with the Emperor's Children Trait gains an additional +1 Combat Resolution Point if all Models in that Unit are not within the Controlling Player's Deployment Zone during the Resolution Sub-Phase. If the Mission being played does not define Deployment Zones, the Controlling Player gains this bonus when not within 12" of their Battlefield Edge, or if no Battlefield Edge is specified, a Battlefield Edge that is selected prior to the First Turn.
rules.name: Sire of the Emperor's Children

rules.description: Fulgrim Transfigured cannot be included in the same Army as Fulgrim
rules.name: Scion of Corruption

rules.description:
Fulgrim Transfigured must have a Challenge declared for him and may use the Heroic Intervention Reaction without expending a Reaction Point.

In the Controlling Player's Challenge Sub-Phase, if a Model with this Special Rule is eligible to have a Challenge declared for it, the Controlling Player must do so. In addition, if the Controlling Player of a Model with this Special Rule declares that they will use the Heroic Intervention Advanced Reaction, and selects a Model with this Special Rule, they are not required to spend any points from their Reaction Allotment to do so.
rules.name: Avatar of Perfection

rules.description:
"Lucius gains an increased benefit from the Paragon of Excellence Gambit."


If the Paragon of Excellence Gambit is selected for a Model with this Special Rule, add a +4 modifier to the Focus Roll instead of +2.
rules.name: The Faultless Blade

rules.description:
"This Gambitallows Saul Tarvitz to gain benefits when fighting specific opponents."


While a Model with this Special Rule is in a Challenge with an enemy Model with the Emperor's Children, World Eaters, Sons of Horus, or Death Guard Traits, its Controlling Player can select the following Gambit:


Bite of the Betrayed: This Gambit can only be selected during the first Face-Off Step of a Challenge. If this Gambit is selected, until this Challenge ends, modify this Model's Weapon Skill, Strength, and Toughness Characteristics by +1.
rules.name: A Brother Betrayed

selectionEntryGroups.name: All models may have one of the following: All must have the same.
May select one of the following
May have its Palatine blade exchanged for one of the following:
May have its Palatine blade exchanged for one of the following:
May have its bolt pistol exchanged for one of the following:

rules.description:
"Rylanor can fight in a Challenge when Fulgrim is the Challenger."
If a Model with this Special Rule is Locked in Combat with an enemy Unit that contains Fulgrim or Fulgrim Transfigured, the Model with this Special Rule is considered to be eligible to take part in a Challenge. In such a Challenge, if Fulgrim or Fulgrim Transfigured is the Challenger, Rylanor must be the Challenged.
rules.name: Vengeful Hate

modifiers.value: Up to two Sun Killers in this Unit may each have one  item from

selectionEntryGroups.name: One Sun Killer in this Unit may have one vexilla selected for it for +10 Points.
May have one item from the Legion Sergeant Melee Weapons list selected for it.
Every Model in the Unit must have one of the following options selected for it. Every Model in this Unit must have the same item selected for it:

rules.description:
"Models with this Special Rule can have their Ballistic Skill improved once per Battle against a specific target."
Once per Battle, before making a Shooting Attack in the Controlling Player’s Shooting Phase, the Controlling Player of a Unit with this Special Rule that did not Move in the previous Movement Phase may select an enemy Unit with the Vehicle Type as that Unit’s Designated Quarry. When the Unit with this Special Rule makes a Shooting Attack that targets an enemy Unit that is selected as their Designated Quarry, the Ballistic Skill Characteristic of all Models with this Special Rule in the Unit making the Shooting Attack is modified by +1 for the duration of the current Phase.
rules.name: Designated Quarry

modifiers.value: Up to two Inductii in this Unit may each have one of

selectionEntryGroups.name: For every four Models in this Unit, one Inductii in this Unit may have its chainsword exchanged for

modifiers.value: For every four Models in this Unit, one Inductii in this Unit may have its bolt pistol exchanged for

rules.description:
"An additional Dice is rolled when making a Check to avoid Tactical Statuses for a Unit containing any Models with this Special Rule, discarding the worst result in the Shooting Phase and the best result in the Combat Phase."


When a Check is made for a Unit containing any Models with this Special Rule to avoid gaining a Tactical Status, the Controlling Player must roll an additional Dice, discarding the Dice which, once rolled, has the highest result if the Check is made in the Morale Sub-Phase, or the Dice which, once rolled, has the lowest result if the Check is made in the Resolution Sub-Phase.
rules.name: Perfection Embodied

rules.description:
"Emperor's Children gain an Initiative bonus when they successfully Charge."


On any Turn in which they made a Charge Move, Models with this Special Rule gain an additional modifier to their Combat Initiative of +1 until the end of the Phase.
rules.name: Flawless Execution

characteristics.$text: This Gambit allows the Controlling Player a bonus to the Focus Roll in the first round of a Challenge.
characteristics.name: Summary

characteristics.$text: This Gambit can only be selected during the first Face-Off Step of a Challenge involving this Model. While this Gambit is selected, during the Focus Step the Controlling Player gains a +2 modifier to the Focus Roll.
characteristics.name: Description

characteristics.$text: This Reaction allows the Reacting Unit to Charge an enemy Unit that has made a Charge.
characteristics.name: Summary

characteristics.$text: Once per Battle, the Reactive Player may declare the Perfect Counter Advanced Reaction at the end of Step 3 of the Charge process of any Charge declared by the Active Player that targets a Unit under that Player's control which only includes Models with the Emperor's Children Trait.
characteristics.name: Trigger

characteristics.$text: The Reactive Player must spend 1 point of their Reaction Allotment to declare the Perfect Counter Advanced Reaction, this cost paid as soon as the declaration is made.
characteristics.name: Cost

characteristics.$text: The Target Unit is always the Unit whose Charge triggered the Reaction. The Reacting Unit is always the Unit that was declared as the target of the Charge that triggered this Reaction.
characteristics.name: Target

characteristics.$text:
1. Once this Reaction is declared, the Reactive Player must make a Charge Roll for the Reacting Unit.
2. If the result of the Charge Roll would be enough to move any Model from the Reacting Unit into Base contact with a Model from the Target Unit the a Charge Move is made and the Units are Locked in Combat. No Model in the Target Unit may claim a bonus from any Special Rule that requires a successful Charge to have been made, while the Models in the Reacting Unit gain any bonuses from any Special Rule that would apply to that Unit as a result of a successful Charge.
3. If the result of the Charge Roll would not bring any Model from the Reacting Unit into Base Contact with a Model from the Target Unit, then no Charge Move is made and the Players proceed to Step 4 of the Charge process.
characteristics.name: Process

rules.description:
"The Controlling Player may choose that a Unit becomes Stupefied when it is the target of a Shooting Attack."


After a Shooting Attack that targets a Unit entirely composed of Models with this Special Rule is resolved, that Unit's Controlling Player can choose for the targeted Unit to become Stupefied.
rules.name: Lords of Profligacy

rules.description:
When a Unit becomes Stupefied, all Tactical Statuses affecting it are removed. In addition, while a Unit is Stupefied:


- Models in that Unit have the Feel No Pain (6+) Special Rule.
- The Strength Characteristic of Models in that Unit is modified by +1.
- Models in that Unit cannot gain Tactical Statuses.
- The Controlling Player of a Unit that is Stupefied may not declare any Reactions for that Unit and must make all Shooting Attacks declared for that Unit as Snap Shots.
- In the Effects Sub-Phase of the Controlling Player's End Phase, the Controlling Player must make a Cool Check for each Unit from their Army that is Stupefied. If the Check is passed, that Unit is no longer Stupefied.
rules.name: Stupefied

characteristics.$text: When this Gambit is selected, the Controlling Player doubles any bonus for Outside Support and also gains a bonus when Stupefied.
characteristics.name: Summary

characteristics.$text: If this Gambit is selected, then the Model gains double the normal modifier for Outside Support during the Focus Step. If the Unit this Model is part of is Stupefied, add an additional modifier of +1.
characteristics.name: Description

characteristics.$text: This Reaction allows the Reacting Unit to become Stupefied when it is Charged.
characteristics.name: Summary

characteristics.$text: The Reactive Player may declare the Twisted Desire Advanced Reaction at the end of Step 2 of a Charge declared by the Active Player that targets a Unit under the Reactive Player's control which only includes Models with the Emperor's Children Trait.
characteristics.name: Trigger

characteristics.$text: The Reactive Player must spend 1 point of their Reaction Allotment to declare the Twisted Desire Advanced Reaction, this cost paid as soon as the declaration is made.
characteristics.name: Cost

characteristics.$text: The Target Unit is always the Unit whose Charge triggered the Reaction. The Reacting Unit is always the Unit that was declared as the target of the Charge that triggered this Reaction.
characteristics.name: Target

characteristics.$text: Once this Reaction is declared, the Reacting Unit becomes Stupefied, and for the duration of this Assault Phase, Models in that Unit gain the Feel No Pain (5+) Special Rule.
characteristics.name: Process

modifiers.value: Legiones Hereticus can only be selected for an Army with the Traitor Allegiance
Existing Warlord Detachment must be removed
Roster must be at least 3000 points for Brotherhood of the Phoenix
Brotherhood of the Phoenix only allowed with Legiones Hereticus Rite of War
Brotherhood of the Phoenix is only valid for Emperor's Children
